name: Greek Easter Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg Yolk plus 1 tablespoon Milk beaten together
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine the butter and powdered sugar. Beat until fluffy.
3. Add the egg, brandy (or milk), and vanilla to the butter mixture. Beat well after each addition.
4. Gradually add the flour mixture into the butter mixture, cup at a time, blending well after each addition.
5. Roll about one teaspoon of dough on a lightly floured surface into a 6-inch rope.
6. Fold the rope in half and twist 2 or 3 times. Pinch the ends together to form a cookie shape.
7. Place the cookies about an inch apart on greased cookie sheets.
8. Brush each cookie with egg/milk mixture.
9. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
10. Bake for 10-13 minutes in a preheated oven at 375°F until golden brown.
11. Cool on a wire rack.
12. Store in an airtight container at room temperature. Makes 4 dozen cookies.
